summary: All 5 round-1 findings have been addressed. The two medium findings are resolved in substance: T7-TEST-01 is materially improved (the static-grep remains the authoritative check and store.size() now provides a live-state assertion) and T7-TEST-02 is superseded by a stronger design — verify.ts migrated to checkAndInsert and the test asserts that API call, not the now-retired has+insert pair. T7-TEST-03 is now a genuinely distinct negative-classification test. T7-TEST-04 and T7-TEST-05 are fully closed. Two new low-severity observations are raised but do not block approval.

T7-TEST-01.assessment: The test now plants an entry via store.checkAndInsert, snapshots mutation-spy counts, runs settleOnChain, and then asserts (a) the spy counts did not increase, (b) store.size() is still 1. The runtime spy assertions are still structurally vacuous in one sense — settle.ts does not accept a nonceStore parameter so the spied store is invisible to it — but this is an inherent consequence of the architectural separation (settle.ts cannot have a reference to a store it never receives). The meaningful guarantees are now: (1) the static grep proving no replay-store import, (2) store.size() surviving the settle call proving no global side-effect, and (3) the delta-count check communicating intent clearly. This is the strongest achievable test for this contract given the separation-of-concerns design. Accepted.

T7-TEST-02.assessment: verify.ts was migrated to checkAndInsert (the production-documented atomic primitive from replay-store.ts). The new test asserts checkAndInsert was called exactly once with the correct from+nonce, and that the test-only has+insert methods were never invoked. This is stronger than the original microtask sentinel: it enforces the API contract (use the TOCTOU-safe primitive), not just the timing property. If someone reverted to has+insert, checkSpy fires 0 times and the test fails. TOCTOU safety is now a property of checkAndInsert itself (its implementation runs has+insert as a single synchronous block), so the test correctly delegates that invariant to the store's own test suite.

T7-TEST-03.assessment: The duplicate 'undecoded revert' test is replaced with a ContractFunctionExecutionError whose cause message contains a generic revert reason (no 'authorization is used' marker). The test asserts gas_estimate_revert and explicitly confirms the classifier does NOT promote to authorization_already_used_onchain. This is a genuinely distinct test path that exercises the negative branch of matchesAuthorizationAlreadyUsed inside classifyWriteError.

T7-TEST-04.assessment: The test now serializes both result and opts and asserts the raw 64-hex key bytes are absent from both. Serializing opts exercises the OpaqueRelayerKey.toJSON() redaction path, which is the actual security invariant the test should enforce. The serialized opts include the relayerKey field; JSON.stringify calls toJSON() on it, which must return '<redacted:relayer-key>' not the raw hex.

T7-TEST-05.assessment: Fence-post test at validBefore == nowSec+5 added. It asserts authorization_expired, confirming the guard is `<=` not strict `<`. Matches the implementation at verify.ts:153.

new_observations.title: Top-of-file docblock still describes the old has+insert pair
new_observations.description: Lines 22 and 29-31 of verify.ts still read 'NonceStore.has → true → nonce_already_used' and 'nonceStore.has(...) is followed synchronously by nonceStore.insert(...)'. The implementation now uses checkAndInsert exclusively, which delegates both steps internally. The comments are factually stale and could mislead a future maintainer about which NonceStore API is the authoritative call site.
new_observations.suggested_fix: Update the docblock comment at point 3 to read '- NonceStore.checkAndInsert → accepted:false → nonce_already_used' and replace point 5 with a sentence describing the TOCTOU guarantee as a property of checkAndInsert.

new_observations.title: No test for HttpRequestError with status < 500 after implementation change
new_observations.description: settle.ts was updated to classify ALL HttpRequestError as rpc_5xx (previously only status >= 500 mapped there; non-5xx mapped to rpc_timeout). The task spec says 'HttpRequestError with status >= 500 → rpc_5xx'. The existing test only covers status 502. With the change, a 429 (rate-limit) or 401 (auth) HttpRequestError now maps to rpc_5xx instead of rpc_timeout. There is no test exercising this case in either direction. This is a test gap introduced by the implementation change — not a pre-existing gap.
new_observations.note: The implementation change itself may be intentional (comment in settle.ts says collapsing all HttpRequestError to rpc_5xx is less misleading for operators). From a test-review standpoint, the gap exists but does not affect the mandatory TDD anchors. A follow-up test at HttpRequestError status 429 would close it.

positive_observations: verify.ts migration to checkAndInsert is the right architectural decision: it delegates the TOCTOU atomicity guarantee to replay-store.ts (which owns that invariant) rather than requiring verify.ts to enforce it structurally.

The T7-TEST-03 replacement correctly tests the negative-classification guard: a ContractFunctionExecutionError without the 'authorization is used' substring must not be promoted to authorization_already_used_onchain. This is the meaningful undecoded-revert test the spec intended.
The fence-post at nowSec+5 (T7-TEST-05) directly validates the `<=` operator. Combined with the nowSec+4/nowSec+6 tests it gives full three-point boundary coverage.

The updated opts serialization in T7-TEST-04 exercises OpaqueRelayerKey.toJSON() — the test now has teeth for the actual secret-leakage vector (JSON.stringify of a config-like object containing the key).
settle.ts new v/r/s yParity normalization guard (early return gas_estimate_revert when neither yParity nor v is present) is a correctness improvement, though currently untested.

tdd_anchor_coverage.note: verify gained 1 additional test (fence-post nowSec+5) for total 15. settle count unchanged at 19. Total task-7 tests: 34.

task_required.reason: No critical or high severity findings. Both remaining observations (T7-R2-01 stale comment, T7-R2-02 missing HttpRequestError < 500 branch) are low severity and do not affect correctness, security, or spec compliance. All mandatory TDD anchors are present and pass. The implementation changes in round 2 (checkAndInsert migration, HttpRequestError classifier simplification, yParity guard) are correctness improvements and the test suite adequately covers the core paths. Approve.
